page: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a virtual server in the High Frequency Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Core Processor (Skylake, IBRS) with 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of memory, providing 1.0 GB per core, and includes 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark results indicate mixed performance characteristics. Single-core performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance scoring 1425.29 (average) and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 24.24 GB/sec (average). However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 2786.09 falls into the weak category, while the PassMark CPU score of 3137.23 is also weak. Memory bandwidth is constrained, with large block read-only bandwidth at 18.67 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 12.65 GB/sec, both ranking in the poor category.

The server's shared CPU allocation and low memory capacity present clear tradeoffs, particularly for database and high-throughput web operations. PassMark database operations scored 687.45, and static web serving throughput reached 2.3 GB/sec, both in the poor category. Redis SET operations achieved 2.3 million operations per second. This instance is configured for entry-level workloads, lightweight single-threaded tasks, basic utility scripting, or small-scale development environments where high memory density and dedicated CPU resources are not required.
description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is an x86_64 virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 2 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 2.0 GB of memory, and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data reveals average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1425.29 and single-threaded gzip compression of 24.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory performance are limited, as shown by a weak stress-ng multi-core score of 2786.09 and poor large-block memory read bandwidth of 18.67 GB/sec. Database operations and web serving throughput also fall into lower performance tiers. Given the shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, this server is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic utility tasks, and development environments rather than memory-intensive or high-throughput database workloads.
og_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of NVMe SSD storage, designed for lightweight workloads and basic utility tasks.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is an x86_64 virtual server with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of NVMe SSD storage for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared virtual server featuring dual Intel vCPUs and local NVMe storage for entry-level, single-threaded computing tasks.
bullet_points: Intel Skylake x86_64 architecture with 2 shared vCPUs at 2.0 GHz
2.0 GB of system memory and 80 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression
Constrained multi-core scaling and memory bandwidth under heavy loads
Suited for lightweight utility scripting, development, and basic web hosting
